name: lookup_funcs
description: Get functions by address or name (auto-detects)

name: int_convert
description: Convert numbers to different formats

size.description: Byte size for conversion (omit for auto)
Byte size for conversion (omit for auto)

inputs.description: Convert numbers to various formats (hex, decimal, binary, ascii)

name: list_funcs
description: List functions

filter.description: Optional glob pattern to filter results

count.description: Maximum number of results (default: 50, 0 for all)

filter.description: Optional glob pattern to filter results

count.description: Maximum number of results (default: 50, 0 for all)

queries.description: List functions with optional filtering and pagination

name: list_globals
description: List globals

filter.description: Optional glob pattern to filter results

count.description: Maximum number of results (default: 50, 0 for all)

filter.description: Optional glob pattern to filter results

count.description: Maximum number of results (default: 50, 0 for all)

queries.description: List global variables with optional filtering and pagination

name: refresh_caches
description: Force-refresh all caches (strings, functions, globals). Call this after bulk modifications to ensure list_funcs/list_globals return fresh data.

name: imports
description: List imports

name: find_regex
description: Search strings with case-insensitive regex patterns

pattern.description: Regex pattern to search for in strings

name: decompile
description: Decompile function to pseudocode

name: disasm
description: Disassemble function to assembly instructions

name: xrefs_to
description: Get cross-references to specified addresses

name: xrefs_to_field
description: Get cross-references to structure fields

name: callees
description: Get functions called by the specified functions

addrs.description: Function addresses to get callees for

name: find_bytes
description: Search for byte patterns in the binary (supports wildcards with ??)

name: basic_blocks
description: Get control flow graph basic blocks for functions

addrs.description: Function addresses to get basic blocks for

max_blocks.description: Max basic blocks per function (default: 1000, max: 10000)

name: find
description: Search for patterns in the binary (strings, immediate values, or references)

name: export_funcs
description: Export function data in various formats

format.description: Export format: json (default), c_header, or prototypes

name: callgraph
description: Build call graph starting from root functions

roots.description: Root function addresses to start call graph traversal from

max_depth.description: Maximum depth for call graph traversal

max_nodes.description: Max nodes across the graph (default: 1000, max: 100000)

max_edges.description: Max edges across the graph (default: 5000, max: 200000)

name: get_bytes
description: Read bytes from memory addresses

addr.description: Address to read from (hex or decimal)
Address to read from (hex or decimal)

name: get_int
description: Read integer values from memory addresses

addr.description: Address to read from (hex or decimal)
Address to read from (hex or decimal)

queries.description: Integer read requests (ty, addr). ty: i8/u64/i16le/i16be/etc

name: get_string
description: Read strings from memory addresses

name: get_global_value
description:
Read global variable values by address or name
    (auto-detects hex addresses vs names)

queries.description: Global variable addresses or names to read values from

name: patch
description: Patch bytes at memory addresses with hex data

name: put_int
description: Write integer values to memory addresses

addr.description: Address to write to (hex or decimal)

value.description: Integer value as string (decimal or 0x..; negatives allowed for signed)

addr.description: Address to write to (hex or decimal)

value.description: Integer value as string (decimal or 0x..; negatives allowed for signed)

items.description: Integer write requests (ty, addr, value). value is a string; supports 0x.. and negatives

name: declare_type
description: Declare types

name: read_struct
description:
Reads struct type definition and parses actual memory values at the
    given address as instances of that struct type.

    If struct name is not provided, attempts to auto-detect from address.
    Auto-detection only works if IDA already has type information applied
    at that address

    Returns struct layout with actual memory values for each field.

name: search_structs
description: Search structs

filter.description: Case-insensitive substring to search for in structure names

name: set_type
description: Apply types (function/global/local/stack)

name: infer_types
description: Infer types

name: set_comments
description: Set comments at addresses (both disassembly and decompiler views)

name: patch_asm
description: Patch assembly instructions at addresses

name: rename
description: Unified rename operation for functions, globals, locals, and stack variables

func_addr.description: Function address containing the local variable
Function address containing the local variable
Function address containing the stack variable
Function address containing the stack variable

name: append_comments
description:
Append comments at addresses, deduping exact text by default. Unlike
    set_comments (which overwrites), this preserves existing annotations — use
    it for incremental commentary. scope='auto' (default) writes a function
    comment when addr is a function start, otherwise a line comment; force
    with scope='func' or 'line'. dedupe=True skips writes when the exact
    stripped text already appears on its own line.

dedupe.description: Skip if exact text already exists (default: true)
Skip if exact text already exists (default: true)

name: define_func
description:
Define a function at each given address. IDA infers bounds unless an
    explicit end address is provided. Returns {addr, start, end} on success or
    {addr, start, error} if the function already exists or add_func fails.
    Use this when IDA auto-analysis missed a function entry point.

end.description: Optional end address for explicit bounds
Optional end address for explicit bounds

name: define_code
description:
Convert raw bytes to a code instruction at each given address. Returns
    {addr, ea, length} on success (length is the instruction byte length) or
    {addr, ea, error} if create_insn failed. Use this when IDA classified an
    instruction as data or failed to decode.

end.description: Optional end address for explicit bounds
Optional end address for explicit bounds

name: undefine
description:
Undefine item(s) at each address, converting them back to raw bytes.
    Size is determined from `end` (exclusive) or `size`; defaults to 1 byte.
    Uses ida_bytes.DELIT_EXPAND so adjacent items spanning into the range are
    fully removed. Returns {addr, start, size} on success.

name: stack_frame
description: Get stack vars

name: declare_stack
description: Create stack vars

name: delete_stack
description: Delete stack vars

name: py_eval
description:
Execute Python code in IDA context.
    Returns dict with result/stdout/stderr.
    Has access to all IDA API modules.
    Supports Jupyter-style evaluation.

name: survey_binary
description:
Get a compact overview of the binary in one call. Returns file metadata,
    segment layout, entry points, statistics, top 15 strings and functions ranked
    by xref count (functions include classification: thunk/wrapper/leaf/dispatcher/
    complex), imports by category, and call graph summary. Use this as your FIRST
    tool call when starting analysis. Do not call list_funcs, imports, or find_regex
    separately for triage — this returns all of that. Use detail_level='minimal'
    for binaries with >10k functions.

name: analyze_function
description:
Compact single-function analysis: pseudocode (capped at 100 lines), top
    strings, top non-trivial constants, callers, callees, xrefs, comments, and
    basic block summary. Use this for "tell me everything about function X" in
    one call instead of chaining decompile + callees + xrefs_to separately.

include_asm.description: Include full disassembly (default: false, saves tokens)

name: analyze_component
description:
Analyze related functions as a group: per-function compact summaries,
    internal call graph (edges only between supplied functions), shared globals,
    interface vs internal classification, and strings used by multiple members.

name: diff_before_after
description:
Apply a rename/type/comment action and return the before/after decompilation
    side by side. Actions: 'rename_func' ({name}), 'set_type' ({type}),
    'set_comment' ({comment}). Useful to verify a rename or type change actually
    improved readability. Returns {before, after, action_applied, changes_detected}.

name: trace_data_flow
description:
Follow cross-references from or to an address, automatically traversing
    multiple hops. 'forward' follows xrefs-from, 'backward' follows xrefs-to.
    Returns nodes (with function name, instruction, code/data classification) and
    edges. Do not use for call graph traversal — use callgraph for that.

name: entity_query
description: Query IDB entities with filtering, projection, sorting, and pagination.

queries.description: Entity query or list of queries. kind=functions|globals|imports|strings|names.

name: search_text
description: Search the rendered IDA listing for disassembly/comment text using IDA's native text search.

pattern.description: Text or regex pattern to search for

name: py_exec_file
description: Execute a Python script file in IDA context and return result/stdout/stderr.

file_path.description: Absolute path to a Python script to execute

name: make_signature
description: Create unique byte signatures for addresses or names.

name: make_signature_for_function
description: Create unique byte signatures for function entry points.

name: make_signature_for_range
description: Create a byte signature for a specific address range.

name: find_xref_signatures
description: Find unique signatures for code locations that reference an address.

top.description: Number of shortest signatures per address

name: type_query
description: Query local types with structured filters, relationships, and pagination.

queries.description: Type query or list of queries

name: type_inspect
description: Inspect named types and optionally include member layout.

queries.description: Type inspection query or list of queries

name: type_apply_batch
description: Apply multiple type edits and return aggregate status.

batch.description: Batch with edits and optional stop_on_error
